name: policyengine-uk-data

metadata.name: policyengine-uk-data
metadata.summary: A package to create representative microdata for the UK.

documentation: # PolicyEngine UK Data

methods.name: policyengine_uk_data.datasets.frs.dwp_frs.DWP_FRS.generate
documentation:
Generate the survey data from the original TAB files.

Args:
    tab_folder (Path): The folder containing the original TAB files.

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.incomes_projection.create_target_matrix
documentation:
Create a target matrix A, s.t. for household weights w, the target vector b and a perfectly calibrated PolicyEngine UK:

A * w = b

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.datasets.sum_to_entity
documentation:
Sums values by joining foreign and primary keys.

Args:
    values (pd.Series): The values in the non-entity table.
    foreign_key (pd.Series): E.g. pension.person_id.
    primary_key ([type]): E.g. person.index.

Returns:
    pd.Series: A value for each person.

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.datasets.categorical
documentation:
Maps a categorical input to an output using given left and right arrays.

Args:
    values (pd.Series): The input values.
    default (int): A default value (to replace NaNs).
    left (list): The left side of the map.
    right (list): The right side of the map.

Returns:
    pd.Series: The mapped values.

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.datasets.sum_from_positive_fields
documentation:
Sum from fields in table, ignoring negative values.

Args:
    table (DataFrame)
    fields (List[str])

Returns:
    np.array

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.datasets.sum_positive_variables
documentation:
Sum positive variables.

Args:
    variables (List[str])

Returns:
    np.array

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.datasets.fill_with_mean
documentation:
Fills missing values in a table with the mean of the column.

Args:
    table (DataFrame): Table to fill.
    code (str): Column signifying existence.
    amount (str): Column with values.
    multiplier (float): Multiplier to apply to amount.

Returns:
    np.array: Filled values.

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.loss.create_target_matrix
documentation:
Create a target matrix A, s.t. for household weights w, the target vector b and a perfectly calibrated PolicyEngine UK:

A * w = b

modules.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.spi
documentation: This script generates SPI-based statistics on income types from public SPI publications.
There are two tables needed:
Profit, employment and pension income: https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/earned-income-2010-to-2011
Property, interest and dividend income: https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/investment-income-2010-to-2011
Minor step to avoid an ods dependency: put the ODS files into a CSV file with the header being the table header, then point the below references to those CSVs.
Python script to extract income statistics from SPI .ods files.

functions.name: policyengine_uk_data.utils.imputations.capital_gains.impute_capital_gains
documentation: Assumes that the capital gains distribution is the same for all years.
